title: Selling Online Without Gumroad: An Honest Side-by-Side Breakdown
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: PayPal and Stripe block creators in dozens of countries. Here is the infrastructure that works everywhere they don't.

content.text: If you’re in Nigeria, Pakistan, or Ghana, Gumroad’s country restrictions lock you out. Stripe and PayPal follow suit, leaving creators stranded without payment options. Even if your country is supported, banking limitations or KYC requirements can still block access. This isn’t just an inconvenience—it’s a career killer for digital creators selling ebooks, courses, or art. Crypto-native infrastructure offers a permanent alternative, but how does it stack up against traditional platforms? Let’s break it down side by side.
What Each Option Actually Offers

Gumroad, Stripe, and PayPal bundle payment processing, file delivery, and basic analytics into one platform. They’re simple but restrictive. Crypto-native tools like Unchained Commerce’s Full Stack Bundle ($79.99) decouple these functions, offering a backend engine for automated delivery, a frontend wallet connector, and an admin dashboard. The tradeoff? You handle your own payment processor (NOWPayments in this case), but you gain global reach and no KYC. Gumroad wins for ease if you’re in a supported country, but crypto-native wins for accessibility everywhere else.
How They Handle Restricted Countries

Gumroad, Stripe, and PayPal exclude entire countries based on regulatory or risk factors. Nigeria, Ghana, and Pakistan are outright blocked. Even if your country is supported, individual accounts can be suspended for arbitrary reasons. Crypto-native infrastructure is borderless by design. Unchained Commerce’s Core Shop Engine ($49.99) works in every country because it doesn’t rely on traditional banking rails. If your buyer can send crypto, you can get paid. Clear winner here—crypto-native tools provide structural inclusion where traditional platforms fail.
Payment Speed, Fees, and Real Costs

Gumroad charges 10% per transaction, Stripe and PayPal take 2.9% + $0.30. Crypto fees vary by network but average 1-3%. The real cost with traditional platforms? Sudden account freezes or fund holds. With crypto, payments settle directly to your wallet—no middleman. Unchained Commerce’s Web3 Wallet Connect ($19.99) supports 300+ coins, so you’re not stuck with high Ethereum gas fees. For speed and control, crypto-native wins, especially if you’re in a restricted country.
Gumroad blocks entire countries, but crypto-native tools work everywhere.
Automation and Delivery Capabilities

Gumroad automates file delivery and sends PDF receipts, but it’s tied to their payment system. Unchained Commerce’s Core Shop Engine ($49.99) does the same but works with any crypto payment. The Admin Dashboard ($29.99) adds transaction logs and real-time analytics. The Full Stack Bundle ($79.99) combines everything, so you get Gumroad-level automation without geographic restrictions. If you’re blocked from traditional platforms, this is your only viable alternative.
Which One to Choose Based on Your Situation

If you’re in a supported country with a stable bank account, Gumroad is simpler. If you’re in Nigeria, Ghana, Pakistan, or any restricted country, crypto-native infrastructure is your only reliable option. Unchained Commerce’s Full Stack Bundle ($79.99) gives you everything you need to sell globally without KYC or banking hurdles. It’s a one-time payment for lifetime access—no subscriptions. If you’re blocked, this is your path forward.
How do I sell digital products if PayPal is not available in my country?

If PayPal isn’t available in your country, crypto-native infrastructure is the best alternative. Unchained Commerce’s Core Shop Engine ($49.99) lets you accept 300+ cryptocurrencies without KYC or a PayPal account. Payments settle directly to your wallet, and automated delivery ensures buyers get their products instantly. It’s a permanent solution for creators in restricted countries.
Traditional platforms freeze accounts—crypto payments settle directly to your wallet.
